name: rubiks_cube_layer_twist_challenge
description: 3x3 Rubik's cube layer manipulation challenge with standard notation (R, L, U, D moves).

initial_state.description: The cube sits on the table in its solved state. All faces show solid colors - white, yellow, red, orange, blue, and green. The cube has not been scrambled yet.

goal_states.description: Success! After two consecutive right face twists (R R sequence), the cube has reached a solved configuration. The color patterns align correctly.
Success! The top face followed by left face twist (U L sequence) quickly solved the cube. This efficient two-move layer sequence is the shortest solution.
Success! Three consecutive right face twists after the top twist (U R R R sequence) solved the cube. This longer layer sequence reached a valid solution.

states.description: The cube sits on the table in its solved state. All faces show solid colors - white, yellow, red, orange, blue, and green. The cube has not been scrambled yet.
The cube has been scrambled and is now in its starting configuration for the challenge. The faces show a mixed pattern of colors. This is the initial puzzle state that needs to be solved.

After the first move, the cube shows a new configuration. Multiple layer twists are possible: right face (R), left face (L), top face (U), or bottom face (D). Each will create a different arrangement of colors and patterns.
The right face has been twisted once (R move applied). The layer rotation created new edge and corner positions. Another right face twist is available to continue this sequence.
Success! After two consecutive right face twists (R R sequence), the cube has reached a solved configuration. The color patterns align correctly.
One right face twist completed (R move). From here, you can twist either the bottom face (D move) or the left face (L move). Different layer choices lead to different outcomes.
Dead end. After twisting right then down (R D sequence), the cube is in an unsolvable configuration for this challenge. The layer combination doesn't lead to any solution.
After right and left layer twists (R then L sequence), the cube has returned to a familiar configuration. The state resembles an earlier branching point with multiple options.
Dead end. The bottom face twist from the initial state (D move) led to a configuration that doesn't connect to any solution path. This layer choice was not productive.

The top face has been twisted (U move applied). The upper layer rotation changed the top edges and corners. From this configuration, three layer options are available: right face (R), left face (L), or a combination move.
After top and right twists (U R sequence). The cube shows the result of these two layer rotations. Another choice: continue with right (R) or twist bottom (D).
Two consecutive right face twists after the top twist (U R R sequence). The layer rotations have significantly changed the configuration. Final decision: one more right twist (R) or bottom twist (D).
Dead end. The sequence U R R D (top, right, right, down) led to an unsolvable configuration. The bottom face twist at this point was the wrong choice.
Success! Three consecutive right face twists after the top twist (U R R R sequence) solved the cube. This longer layer sequence reached a valid solution.
Success! The top face followed by left face twist (U L sequence) quickly solved the cube. This efficient two-move layer sequence is the shortest solution.
Dead end. After the top twist, the left-bottom combination (U then L D) created an unsolvable state. This layer sequence doesn't lead to solutions.

actions.description: Pick up the cube and scramble it. The solved cube is mixed into a starting configuration for the challenge.
Observe the scrambled cube configuration. Examine the current color arrangement on all visible faces before making your first move.
Twist the right face 90° clockwise (R move). The right layer rotates, changing the edge and corner pieces on that face.
Twist the right face 90° clockwise again (R move). Second consecutive right face rotation.
Twist the top face 90° anticlockwise (U' move). The upper layer rotates counterclockwise, changing the top edges and corners.
Twist the bottom face 90° clockwise (D move). The bottom layer rotates, affecting the lower edges and corners.
Twist the left face 90° clockwise (L move). The left layer rotates, changing pieces on that side.
Continue to next configuration. The cube state cycles back to an earlier branching point.
Twist the right face 90° anticlockwise (R' move). The right layer rotates counterclockwise, changing the edge and corner pieces on that face.
Twist the top face 90° anticlockwise (U' move). The upper layer rotates counterclockwise, changing the top edges and corners.
Twist the right face 90° clockwise (R move). Another right face layer rotation.
Twist the right face 90° clockwise again (R move). Second consecutive right layer twist in this sequence.
Twist the bottom face 90° clockwise (D move). Rotate the bottom layer downward.
Twist the right face 90° clockwise for the third time (R move). Third consecutive right layer rotation completes this sequence.
Twist the left face 90° clockwise (L move). Direct left layer rotation from this configuration.
Twist the left face 90° clockwise followed by bottom face 90° clockwise (L then D moves). Combined layer rotation sequence.

success_strategies.name: Triple Right Path
success_strategies.path: s0 → s1 → s5_track4 → s6_track4_1 → s7_track4_1_again → s_success_3
